authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator and this official guide directly covers total-price and mandatory-fee treatment for short-term lodging.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fee, rate, package, and guest-impact intake

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal ADA Title III authority and its lodging reservation provisions affect accessible-room reservation promises that can become fee, folio, or service-recovery exceptions.

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for credit-card billing-error guidance and is authoritative for dispute timing and consumer protections relevant to duplicate charges and missing refund complaints.

authoritative_sources.title: What kind of records should I keep
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS is the U.S. federal tax authority and its public recordkeeping guidance defines source-document discipline for gross receipts, invoices, credit-card records, and business evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 848: A Guide to Sales Tax for Hotel and Motel Operators
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York State Department of Taxation and Finance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official state tax publication is directly relevant to hotel occupancy tax, taxable rent, exemption support, and jurisdiction-sensitive tax exceptions in lodging workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Tax exception and close blocker handling

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform System of Accounts - Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HFTP publishes USALI, a recognized lodging accounting framework, and the public FAQ clarifies lodging revenue concepts such as room revenue, ADR, packages, rebates, and minimum-stay scenarios.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Lodging revenue terminology and classification support

authoritative_sources.title: Are call center environments considered sensitive areas for PCI DSS Requirement 9.1.1?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body and constrains payment-card handling in contact-center, folio, payment, and refund workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payment security and card-data stop condition review

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public fraud-control reference for suspicious identity and account-use indicators affecting refunds, direct bill, and guest-account changes.

workflow_stages.name: Lodging revenue intake and source-record identification
workflow_stages.description: Identify property, jurisdiction, guest or account type, reservation, stay dates, rate plan, package, folio, payment, tax, and night-audit records before evaluating the financial outcome.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Charge, rate, package, tax, and payment support review
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether PMS folio lines, reservation changes, package components, rate codes, fee disclosures, payment batches, deposits, authorizations, tax lines, and approved adjustments support the requested charge or correction.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation and night-audit exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Compare PMS, booking, POS, payment gateway, revenue-audit, tax engine, and accounting-interface records to identify matched items, posting gaps, unexplained variances, and close blockers.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Customer-impact, dispute, fraud, and policy-exception review
workflow_stages.description: Separate ordinary folio reconciliation from refund exceptions, cardholder disputes, identity mismatch, safety issues, guest harm, or policy exceptions that require adjacent owner action.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closeout, escalation, and materialization packaging
workflow_stages.description: Produce a folio reconciliation summary, night-audit exception note, billing readiness summary, or escalation packet without certifying accounting, tax, legal, fraud, safety, or final approval outcomes.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: reservation confirmations, change history, booking-channel records, rate codes, rate plans, and cancellation terms
PMS folio line items, taxes, fees, deposits, incidental holds, payment tenders, refunds, transfers, direct-bill and city-ledger references
package component maps, outlet and POS transfer details, breakfast or amenity inclusion records, and package breakage support
payment gateway batches, authorization and capture records, chargeback or billing-dispute references, and processor exception reports
night-audit exception reports, cashier reports, revenue audit work queues, accounting-interface statuses, and close blocker logs
tax exemption certificates or support, tax-engine configuration references, occupancy-tax workpapers, and jurisdiction-specific policy extracts
manager adjustment approvals, guest-service recovery notes, refund authority records, fraud or loss-prevention case references, and safety incident flags
systems_of_record: central reservation system and booking-channel records
payment gateways, processor portals, and chargeback platforms
revenue audit tools and night-audit exception queues
accounting interface, general ledger staging, direct-bill, and city-ledger systems
CRM, guest-service recovery, fraud or loss-prevention, and safety incident systems

allowed: Assess whether source documentation supports charges, postings, payment status, package components, tax evidence needs, dispute support, or adjustment routing.
Prepare folio reconciliation summaries, night-audit exception notes, billing readiness summaries, and escalation packets tied to authoritative records.
Segment high-volume revenue exceptions by evidence readiness, close blocker, leakage risk, customer impact, and owner.
escalate: Required property, jurisdiction, tax, PMS, payment gateway, approval, or tenant policy facts are missing.
Folio, reservation, package, payment, or tax records conflict and the difference changes the financial outcome.
Refund, rate override, write-off, manager adjustment, chargeback response, tax interpretation, accounting treatment, fraud, loss-prevention, safety, or legal authority is required.
Public guidance, tenant policy, brand standards, booking-channel terms, processor rules, or jurisdiction-specific rules conflict.
refuse: Requests to invent adjustments, revenue codes, tax treatment, write-offs, or accounting entries without source evidence and authority.
Requests to override pricing, refund, safety, or fraud controls without documented approval.
Requests to certify final accounting, tax, legal, fraud, chargeback, or safety outcomes.

domain_failure_modes: A folio charge is treated as supported even though reservation, package, POS, or payment records do not materially agree.
A refund, rate override, no-show waiver, or manager adjustment is processed without documented authority.
Night audit is forced closed by inventing a correction rather than escalating missing tax, payment, or posting evidence.
A guest-impacting dispute is handled as ordinary revenue work despite fraud, safety, accessibility, or policy-exception implications.
A generic billing answer ignores lodging-specific artifacts such as PMS folio, package components, city ledger, incidental holds, no-show charges, and occupancy-tax exceptions.

required_workflow_questions.question: What booking, order, payment, or guest records govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Reservation confirmations, booking-channel records, rate plans, package components, PMS folios, stay events, payment gateway records, direct-bill or city-ledger records, tax support, approval logs, dispute files, and night-audit exceptions.

required_workflow_questions.question: What refund, pricing, or policy constraints limit the workflow?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Cancellation and no-show terms, rate code authority, package inclusion rules, disclosed mandatory fees, refund thresholds, manager-adjustment limits, tax exemption evidence, cardholder dispute rules, PCI card-data controls, fraud stop conditions, and tenant approval matrices.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and pricing, safety, or exception authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at evidence-backed support, reconciliation, and escalation packaging. Pricing, refund approval, write-off approval, accounting signoff, tax interpretation, fraud adjudication, chargeback response, safety decisions, and guest-service recovery approvals belong to adjacent owners unless explicit delegated authority is retrieved.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Folio charges conflict with reservation, package, or payment records
A refund or rate override is requested without documented authority
Night audit finds unresolved posting or tax exceptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific lodging tax and exemption treatment
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The pack requires tenant retrieval of jurisdiction and tax policy and escalates tax interpretation or certification rather than guessing from the public baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific PMS configuration, rate hierarchy, and adjustment authority
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The pack treats these as required tenant facts and blocks or escalates when they are unavailable.
The pack requires explicit conflict capture and orchestrator return when sources materially change the outcome.

research_constraints_applied: Workflow claims are limited to source-supported reconciliation, evidence review, exception packaging, and boundary handling.
The final spec refuses unsupported financial changes and does not claim formal accounting, tax, legal, fraud, safety, or approval authority.
Required domain scenario families are mapped to scenario IDs and evaluation coverage.
